hero.name: Daniel Yosep
hero.title: Engineering Manager and Software Architect
hero.intro: I design and lead scalable digital products, bridging strong engineering execution with product impact across backend, platform, and AI initiatives.

about.title: Building reliable systems and growing teams that deliver consistently
about.summary: Engineering Manager with hands-on experience in designing and building scalable backend systems, leading cross-functional teams, and fostering strong engineering practices. Focused on practical software architecture, workflow automation, and the thoughtful adoption of AI to enhance developer productivity

experience.label: Experience
experience.description: Started my professional journey in July 2015 and have continued growing through backend engineering, platform work, and people leadership.

position.label: Current Position
position.description: I lead engineering execution with a focus on clarity, reliability, and outcomes that matter for the business and the team.

company.label: Current Company
company.description: I currently work at Kawan Lama Group, where I help teams build dependable systems and improve delivery quality.

philosophy.label: Philosophy
philosophy.description:
Great software is not measured by how much one engineer can build, but by how well a team can sustain, evolve, and improve it over time.

I believe technology should solve business problems—not become the problem itself. That's why I encourage engineers to stay technology-agnostic, embrace continuous learning, and choose the right tools for each challenge.

My mission is to build scalable platforms, grow adaptable engineering teams, and create systems that continue delivering value long after their original authors have moved on.

funFact.label: Fun Fact
funFact.description: I enjoy exploring espresso brewing and the craft behind a good cup of coffee.

career.title: Roles, platforms, and teams shaped across a decade
career.summary: From hands-on backend delivery to engineering management, each role expanded my scope across telecom, insurance, and retail platforms while keeping the focus on reliable systems and practical execution.

timeline.company: PT Toys Game Indonesia (Kawan Lama Group)
timeline.overview: Lead enterprise platform modernization across six business units, balancing engineering leadership, platform architecture, and delivery governance for nationwide retail operations.
responsibilities: Lead a cross-functional team of backend and frontend engineers across multiple retail platforms.
Own hiring, architecture planning, sprint planning, release governance, performance reviews, and stakeholder management.
Guide engineers toward technology-agnostic problem solving and stronger delivery discipline.
achievements: Led Project Ganesha, an enterprise AI platform built around LibreChat, Gemini, Bedrock PoCs, Chainlit PoCs, and Keycloak SSO.
Architected retail and inventory platforms supporting 2,510 stores, 12,587 internal users, and 18-22 million synchronized records per day.
Delivered major cost and risk reductions through observability modernization, infrastructure changes, and cycle count platform design.

timeline.overview: Joined early to help establish Roojai's engineering team in Indonesia, building insurance products, core pricing capabilities, and integration foundations.
responsibilities: Led a small product engineering team spanning backend and frontend delivery.
Defined architecture and engineering standards during the early growth phase of the Indonesia team.
Built digital insurance flows and coordinated cross-system integrations.
achievements: Built a digital insurance registration platform using Vue.js and Node.js.
Designed and implemented the Radar Pricing Engine for quotations, risk assessment, and premium pricing.
Integrated Salesforce, Sompo TPA services, and Xendit payment capabilities into the platform.

timeline.role: Software Engineer -> Supervisor Software Engineer -> Engineering Manager
timeline.overview: Progressed from individual contributor to engineering manager while leading digital transformation across billing, finance, payment, OSS, HR, warehouse, and customer platforms.
responsibilities: Led a cross-functional team covering backend, frontend, mobile, and QA delivery.
Worked closely with finance, HR, sales, network, customer service, and infrastructure stakeholders.
Owned architecture planning, hiring, release governance, incident management, and technical mentoring.
achievements: Architected an enterprise payment platform processing around 10,000-12,000 transactions per day across 13+ payment channels.
Delivered OSS auto-provisioning and multiple enterprise platforms for finance, HR, and warehouse operations.
Modernized software delivery through Jenkins CI/CD, containerization, ETL automation, and enterprise integrations with SAP and Amdocs.

timeline.overview: Started with internal business systems work, focusing on reporting, payroll, database performance, and moving business logic closer to application code.
responsibilities: Built internal reporting and payroll features for business operations.
Worked directly with SQL queries and stored procedure heavy systems.
Supported early maintenance and optimization work across internal applications.
achievements: Developed a POS reporting system using C#.
Contributed to a payroll management system and query performance improvements.
Helped migrate business logic from SQL Server stored procedures into application code.

skills.title: A broad engineering toolkit shaped by enterprise delivery
skills.summary: My experience spans backend systems, platform architecture, integrations, observability, and engineering leadership. These skill groups reflect the tools and practices I have applied across telecom, insurance, retail, and AI platform work.

categories.title: Backend
categories.description: Hands-on backend implementation across enterprise systems, integrations, pricing engines, and platform services.

categories.title: Frontend
categories.description: Product-facing web interfaces for insurance and internal enterprise workflows, with Vue-based implementation experience.

categories.title: Database
categories.description: Designing and operating transactional and operational data layers for enterprise platforms and internal tooling.

categories.title: Infrastructure
categories.description: Delivery pipelines, container platforms, observability, and production operations for business-critical systems.

categories.title: Architecture
categories.description: Platform design patterns and integration approaches used to build scalable, resilient, and maintainable systems.

categories.title: Leadership
categories.description: Engineering leadership practices focused on team growth, delivery clarity, hiring, and architecture decision-making.

education.title: Academic foundation in computer science
education.summary: My formal education established the technical fundamentals that support my work in software engineering, systems thinking, and platform architecture.

entry.summary: Completed undergraduate study in computer science at BINUS, building the foundation for professional work in software engineering and enterprise systems.

projects.title: Case studies from enterprise delivery and personal builds
projects.summary: Most of my projects are enterprise initiatives built in private environments. This section presents anonymized, impact-focused case studies based on real delivery outcomes, plus one personal project currently in progress.
projects.confidentialityNote: Some projects are intentionally anonymized or marked private due to company confidentiality and internal security policies.

items.title: Enterprise AI Platform
items.description: Enterprise AI platform enabling secure internal AI adoption through controlled access, SSO, and practical workflows for engineering productivity.
items.role: Engineering Manager with hands-on experience leading enterprise platform modernization while contributing to solution architecture, AI initiatives, and engineering delivery.
challenges: Balancing enterprise security controls with practical day-to-day usability.
Designing a platform model that can evolve with rapidly changing AI tooling.
achievements: Enabled secure enterprise AI usage with centralized authentication and governance.
Improved team productivity through reusable AI-assisted engineering workflows.

items.title: Enterprise Retail Operations Platform
items.description: Core retail operations platform supporting multi-business execution across store operations, workflows, and operational data consistency.
items.role: Platform architect and delivery lead for architecture decisions, prioritization, and cross-team execution.
challenges: Maintaining consistency across diverse workflows and multiple business units.
Scaling platform reliability for nationwide retail operations.
achievements: Supported operations across 2,510 stores and 6 business units.
Enabled usage by 12,587 internal users through centralized platform services.

items.title: Inventory Synchronization and Cycle Count Platform
items.description: Event-driven inventory platform for high-volume synchronization and cycle count processing across distributed retail operations.
items.role: System architect responsible for architecture design, async processing strategy, and data flow reliability.
challenges: Handling high-throughput asynchronous synchronization reliably at scale.
Preserving data consistency across distributed operational contexts.
achievements: Processed 18-22 million synchronized records per day.
Operated on 24 topics with 2.1+ million asynchronous messages.

items.title: Enterprise Payment Platform
items.description: Enterprise-grade payment platform integrating multiple payment channels and reconciliation flows for high-volume transactions.
items.role: Architecture and integration lead across payment channel onboarding, platform design, and delivery governance.
challenges: Managing reliability across many external payment integrations.
Improving reconciliation quality while reducing manual operational work.
achievements: Processed around 10K-12K transactions per day.
Integrated 13+ payment channels in one enterprise payment ecosystem.

items.title: E-commerce OS (Personal Project)
items.description: Personal e-commerce operating system focused on foundational commerce workflows such as catalog, pricing, and order operations.
items.role: Founder and full-stack builder, handling architecture, implementation, and product iteration.
challenges: Balancing product iteration speed with maintainable architecture decisions.
Defining MVP scope while designing for future extensibility.
achievements: Established core product direction and foundational architecture.
Actively progressing through implementation milestones.
